title: <div> * Trends: The market is stabilizing but remains a seller's market due to l
content:
* Trends: The market is stabilizing but remains a seller's market due to low supply. High mortgage rates (hovering around 6-7%) have created a "lock-in" effect where homeowners are unwilling to sell and lose their low rates, keeping inventory artificially low.
* Pricing:
* NJ: Seeing steady appreciation (approx. 5-6% YoY). Suburban demand remains high as buyers priced out of NYC look west.
* NYC: More volatile. Manhattan prices are stabilizing, while "outer borough" areas (Brooklyn/Queens) see higher competition.
* Agent Pain Point: With fewer transactions per agent, the cost of doing business is under scrutiny. Agents are cutting spend on low-ROI lead gen tools and looking for retention/efficiency tools.
B. The Rental Market: A Regulatory Minefield
* Trends: Rents are at record highs, but growth is slowing. The real story is regulation.
* New York: The "Good Cause Eviction" Law (2024) has fundamentally changed property management, requiring landlords/agents to justify lease non-renewals and capping rent increases (Inflation + 5% or 10%, whichever is lower).
* New Jersey: Strict new Lead-Based Paint Inspection laws require municipalities to inspect rentals every turnover or every three years.
